examples.title: Algorithm: JSP Translation Process
examples.description: Demonstrates how JSP pages are converted into servlets by the container
examples.code:
ALGORITHM JSPtoServlet
BEGIN
 1. Receive HTTP request for JSP page
 2. Check if corresponding servlet exists and is up-to-date
 3. IF not found or outdated THEN
 a. Parse JSP file into Java servlet source code
 b. Compile generated servlet source
 c. Load compiled servlet class
 4. Initialize servlet instance (if new)
 5. Execute service() method to handle request
 6. Return generated HTML response
END
examples.explanation: Steps 3a-3c show the translation phase. Step 5 handles request processing using standard servlet lifecycle.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for initial compilation, O(1) per subsequent request
examples.spaceComplexity: O(m) where m is JSP page complexity

examples.title: Dynamic Time Display
examples.description: JSP page showing server time using scriptlet and expression
examples.code:
<%@ page contentType="text/html;charset=UTF-8" %> Time JSP

Current Time:

<% java.util.Date now = new java.util.Date(); String time = "Server time: " + now.toString(); %>

<%= time %>
examples.explanation: <% %> encloses Java code (scriptlet). <%= %> outputs expression value. Page directive sets content type.

examples.title: Page Directive Configuration
examples.description: JSP page importing Java classes and setting content type
examples.code:
<%@ page import="java.util.Date, java.text.SimpleDateFormat" %> <%@ page contentType="text/html;charset=UTF-8" %> <% SimpleDateFormat sdf = new SimpleDateFormat("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"); String formattedDate = sdf.format(new Date()); %>

Formatted Date: <%= formattedDate %>
examples.explanation: Page directive imports classes and sets MIME type. Scriptlet processes data, expression outputs result.

examples.title: JavaBean Integration
examples.description: Using jsp:useBean to access a Java object in session scope
examples.code:
Page Visits:

<% counter.increment(); %>

Count: <%= counter.getCount() %>
examples.explanation: useBean either creates new Counter or retrieves existing from session. Method calls modify/display state.
